deep_dive.executive_summary: At $13.03 (2026-07-17 close), the base case yields 7.5% per year over 7 years on normalized FY2025 owner cash of $0.99 per share. CleanSpark is a scaled U.S. bitcoin miner (45.6 EH/s, 4.3% network share) pivoting toward AI data centers while carrying $1.79 billion of long-term debt and a growing convert stack. FY2025 owner economics were strong, but H1 FY2026 mining revenue fell year over year and GAAP losses returned when bitcoin marks moved against the treasury book. Marvin proposes watch until either price offers a mid-teens annual return on normalized owner cash or filings show durable ...

components.label: Operating business (fallback)
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
components.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: Net income moved +150.0% versus the prior period (145,777.0 to 364,464.0).
Operating income moved +114.0% versus the prior period (149,043.0 to 318,949.0).
Issued $1.15B zero-coupon convertible notes due 2032; proceeds for power/land, data-center build, share repurchase, and debt repayment
Acquired 271 acres in Austin County, Texas with 285 MW long-term power agreements for future HPC/AI data center campus
Hired Jeffrey Thomas as SVP of AI Data Centers; announced Submer modular AI cooling collaboration
Launched bitcoin treasury / Digital Asset Management (Spot+) strategy using covered calls on held BTC
Bitmain purchase agreements allowing bitcoin-denominated miner payments with repurchase options
FY2024 scale-up: average hashrate rose to 27.6 EH/s; mining revenue $379M (+125% YoY)
Reduced miner useful life from five years to three years, accelerating depreciation on fleet
Griid infrastructure acquisition expanded Georgia footprint (disclosed in FY2025 10-K segment narrative)
Pivoted to large-scale U.S. bitcoin mining after China ban; average hashrate 9.6 EH/s in FY2023
Exited microgrid/solar software legacy; consolidated as pure-play bitcoin miner

industry.structure: Bitcoin mining is a global, commodity-like hash-rate race: four large U.S. operators (including CleanSpark at ~4.3% of network hash) compete on power cost, machine efficiency, and capital access. ASIC supply is concentrated (Bitmain).
industry.share_shift: CleanSpark grew average hash from 9.6 EH/s (FY2023) to 45.6 EH/s (FY2025) but network difficulty rose faster; H1 FY2026 mining revenue fell 7.7% YoY despite scale, signaling hashprice pressure.
industry.trend: Post-2024 halving economics plus rising AI power demand are pushing miners toward HPC hosting pivots and bitcoin treasury strategies; GAAP earnings swing with ASC 350-60 fair-value marks.
